title: Verify runtime enforcement of MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE=0.60 against post-restart live trades

context.trigger: Trades 223 and 224 opened with confidence 0.3353 and 0.3996, both below the new 0.60 floor, both 3h+ after the confirmed restart that was supposed to pick up MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE_001.
context.min_entry_confidence_task: MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE_001 APPROVED — gate is FIRST in check_entry_gates() waterfall in core/risk.py

allowed_files: trades_sports.db (SELECT only: trades WHERE id IN (223, 224))
forbidden_files: Any file not in allowed_files list
questions: 1. Exact restart time — confirm or correct 2026-04-10T19:40:43Z
2. Trade 223 and 224 timestamps from DB — BEFORE or AFTER restart
3. Does bridge entry path call check_entry_gates()? (bot_core.py)
4. Is MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE=0.60 loaded in live runtime? (.env + risk.py)
5. Any log lines showing confidence_too_low rejections after restart?
6. Most likely root cause if low-confidence trades opened post-restart
acceptance_criteria: No code modified, no DB writes, no restarts
Restart time confirmed or corrected with log evidence
Explicit BEFORE/AFTER verdict for trades 223 and 224
Explicit YES/NO on whether bridge entries use check_entry_gates()
Explicit YES/NO on whether 0.60 floor is loaded in live env/code
Explicit conclusion on whether a real bug exists
